 {"id":"bd-cfm1","title":"Printf DISC-001: Implement banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)","description":"Implement IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) for %.0f and similar precision-limited float formatting. Currently uses round-half-away-from-zero which diverges from glibc.

Reference: sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) should return "2" not "3".
Spec: C11 7.21.6.1 footnote says rounding unspecified but POSIX requires IEEE 754 conformance which specifies round-half-to-even.","acceptance_criteria":"- sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 1.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 3.5) returns "4"
- All printf conformance tests pass including round_half_even
- DISC-001 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:00Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Changed format_f and format_e in printf.rs to use round_ties_even() instead of round(). Added test_format_float_bankers_rounding test. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","printf"]}
 {"id":"bd-cfm2","title":"Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)","description":"Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.","acceptance_criteria":"- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0
- All scanf hex float conformance tests pass
- DISC-004 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:01Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Implemented scan_hex_float in scanf.rs to parse hex floats. Added 5 unit tests covering basic, integer, negative, negative exponent, and uppercase cases. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","scanf"]}

 {"id":"bd-cj0","title":"Syscall core: introduce raw Linux x86_64 syscall veneer (no libc::syscall)","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Add a minimal raw syscall veneer module with typed wrappers for read/write/openat/close/mmap/munmap/mprotect/futex/clone/exit.
- No libc::syscall usage in replacement profile paths.
- Invariants doc: argument marshalling, errno handling, restart semantics.

Acceptance:
- Symbol-level tests confirm wrappers return glibc-compatible errno semantics.
- rg check proves replacement profile call graph contains zero libc::syscall invocations.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.109281820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:47.599408Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:47.599408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","p0-path","syscall"]}
 {"id":"bd-cl5u","title":"Conformance: add execute_* handlers for setjmp_ops family (setjmp, _setjmp, longjmp)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:04:53.752782320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:06:13.380046269Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:06:13.378170309Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-clua","title":"resolv: add host-parity ABI probes for synchronous-only async-DNS wrappers","description":"Capture concrete glibc behavior for getaddrinfo_a/gai_error/gai_cancel/gai_suspend degenerate cases in focused ABI probes so future resolver stub work is grounded in observed host semantics instead of assumptions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:54.963463136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:54.963463136Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -699,7 +699,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1101,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-k59i","title":"syscall: pidfd_open invalid pid should dominate invalid flags with EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for pidfd_open(-1, 1), so the invalid-pid contract remains EINVAL even when flags are also invalid. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:57:21.928899667Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:57:21.928899667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-k6ja","title":"syscall: timerfd_settime abstime veneer","description":"Add typed timerfd itimerspec surface in frankenlibc-core and focused syscall veneer tests for TFD_TIMER_ABSTIME, old-value capture, and invalid timerfd_settime pointer paths.
